security_gap.gap_description: Vote transactions could be spoofed without proper authentication/signature verification.

actor_risk.description: The external user interacts directly with the contract via transactions.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Casting votes is a critical governance action; weak authentication allows impersonation.

assumptions: The governance contract does not adequately verify the identity of the voter submitting the transaction.
An attacker can forge transaction details or signatures.

mitigation.strategy: Implement cryptographic signature verification for all vote transactions.

description: An attacker impersonates a legitimate user to submit fraudulent votes to the governance contract, altering proposal outcomes.

security_gap.gap_description: Vote data could be altered during submission or processed incorrectly if input validation is weak.

actor_risk.description: The external user controls the input data sent to the contract.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Tampering with vote data directly compromises the integrity of the governance process.

assumptions: The governance contract does not perform sufficient validation on the structure or content of the vote data.
Vote messages are not protected against modification in transit.

mitigation.strategy: Implement strict input validation and ensure vote data integrity using cryptographic means.

description: An attacker modifies the content of a vote transaction (e.g., changes the vote choice or delegate) before it is processed by the governance contract.

security_gap.gap_description: Lack of irrefutable proof that a specific user submitted a specific vote.

actor_risk.description: The external user performs actions that need to be attributable.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Users may deny their voting actions, potentially disrupting governance accountability.

assumptions: Vote submission is not recorded immutably or cryptographically linked to the voter's identity.
Transaction records on-chain are not considered sufficient proof.

mitigation.strategy: Ensure all votes are signed transactions recorded on the blockchain.

description: A user casts a vote on a proposal but later denies having done so, potentially to avoid consequences or responsibilities related to the outcome.

security_gap.gap_description: The governance contract or network infrastructure could be overwhelmed by malicious voting activity.

actor_risk.description: The external user can initiate potentially high-volume transactions.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Preventing legitimate users from voting disrupts the democratic governance process.

assumptions: The governance contract or blockchain network lacks effective rate limiting or spam prevention for vote submissions.
Processing vote transactions is computationally expensive or resource-intensive.

mitigation.strategy: Implement transaction rate limits, robust input validation, and monitor network/contract load.

description: An attacker floods the network with excessive vote transactions, preventing legitimate users from casting their votes or delaying the processing of governance proposals.

security_gap.gap_description: Vulnerabilities in vote weight calculation or delegation logic could grant undue influence.

actor_risk.description: The external user aims to gain unauthorized influence over governance.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Exploiting voting power calculation directly undermines the BGT-based governance model.

assumptions: The governance contract has a flaw in how it reads BGT balances or delegated voting power.
An attacker can trick the contract into assigning incorrect vote weight.

mitigation.strategy: Rigorously test and audit vote weight calculation and delegation logic.

description: An attacker exploits a vulnerability in the governance contract to cast votes with a voting weight higher than their legitimate BGT stake or delegated power.
